concerns.text: FR-011 mandates identifying the 'most abundant halide in the available data' before generating simulated data. Task T012 describes the simulation formula but omits the explicit step to calculate and record the most abundant halide, leaving a gap in satisfying the spec's fallback logic.

FR-013 requires a 'Physical Plausibility Check' verifying the *sign* of the top feature's coefficient aligns with first-principles electrostatics (e.g., positive charge density → increased affinity). Task T022 only asks to verify alignment with 'electrostatics' generically, missing the specific requirement to validate the direction (sign) against the defined physics model.

FR-009 explicitly forbids Paired Wilcoxon and mandates 'Bootstrap Confidence Intervals (95% CI, 10,000 resamples)' for comparing R²/RMSE. Task T025 correctly forbids Wilcoxon but fails to explicitly mandate the *implementation* of the 10,000-resample bootstrap for the comparison, risking an incomplete implementation of the statistical rigor requirement.

SC-006 requires that if sample size < 10, the CI width must be reported as the specific string/format 'wide'. Task T026 only requires reporting the analysis as 'underpowered' and focusing on CI width, omitting the specific output formatting requirement for the 'wide' flag.

The Spec's Assumptions section explicitly states that 'No validated questionnaires... so the measurement validity requirement... does not apply'. Task T029 (final report generation) does not include a step to explicitly document this exclusion in the report, risking a reviewer query on why the 'Verified Accuracy' gate was not discussed regarding dataset validity.
